name: Suspicious Host File Deletion Activity
notes: This rule triggers when host files are deleted from a system.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Linux File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysinternals SDelete File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Prefetch File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Delete Command, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Delete Application Log
when the event matches Process Path (custom) is not any of C:\Windows\System32\SearchProtocolHost.exe
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Host File Deletion Activity on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Linux File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysinternals SDelete File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Prefetch File Deletion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Delete Command, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Delete Application Log
AND when the event matches Process Path (custom) is not any of C:\Windows\System32\SearchProtocolHost.exe
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Host File Deletion Activity
Event description: Detects when host files are deleted from a system.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
